title: The GLEAM Ecosystem
text: GLEAM MODEL analytical heart ecosystem, designed around scientific robustness, transparency, policy relevance. applies spatially explicit, life cycle-based framework quantify environmental impacts across livestock supply chains assess mitigation options scenarios. gleam R package (resource) provides full access core model equations documentation. supports custom workflows, batch processing, advanced analytics environmental impacts livestock agrifood systems. enables reproducible research, transparent documentation assumptions, easy integration analytical tools. GLEAM-X Interactive Web Application user-friendly interface R package. enables scenario building comparison, easily accessible analysis visualization interpretation results. application designed policymakers, analysts, technical users explore results inform decision-making.

title: The History of GLEAM
text: GLEAM builds nearly two decades FAO work assessing environmental impacts livestock systems global scale. Key milestones include: 2006: Pre-GLEAM era non-spatial global assessments. 2010–2013: Launch GLEAM v1 spatially explicit LCA using 2005 reference-year data. 2017: Release GLEAM v2 GLEAM-interactive web tool. 2023: Release GLEAM v3 2015 reference-year data, based 2019 IPCC Refinement, GLEAM Dashboard. 2024: GLEAM-Water. first global assessment water use livestock agri-food systems 2024–present: GLEAM-X initiative delivering open-source R package interactive web application. history GLEAM model different versions major releases

title: About gleam modules
text: vignette provides high-level overview GLEAM modules, sequence functions, input output datasets. overview full pipeline provided GLEAM Overview. Full documentation module available via module reference pages linked section .

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_demographic_herd_module() full documentation. module takes herd- cohort-level demographic inputs estimates steady-state sex–age herd structure compatible downstream calculations GLEAM. addition cohort population sizes, derives population growth rates offtake numbers. steady state defined constant sex–age cohort structure time, population size potentially growing declining constant rate. module operates steady-state assumption: demographic parameters constant time, population converges stable cohort composition constant annual growth rate. regime reached, model computes cohort population sizes (start/end/average), cohort shares, offtake totals. key feature implementation applies demography daily resolution. Annual mortality offtake inputs converted daily hazards daily transition probabilities competing risks (death vs. offtake vs. survival). Conceptually, corresponds steady-state demographic approach implemented Dynmod STEADY1 (Lesnoff, 2013), adapted daily time-step formulation. population divided sex (female/male) age class (juvenile/subadult/adult), represented six cohorts: FJ, FS, FA (female juvenile, subadult, adult) MJ, MS, MA (male juvenile, subadult, adult).

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_weights_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level live weight metrics combining cohort-level inputs herd-level biological parameters. module appends cohort weights (initial, potential final, slaughter), derives average final live weights accounting offtake, finally computes average daily live weight gain cohort stage. calculation pipeline consists following steps: Cohort-stage weight assignment — herd-level biological parameters matched cohort row herd_id weights assigned per life stage using calc_cohort_weights(). Average final live weights (accounting offtake) — computed using calc_avg_weights(). Average daily live weight gain — computed using calc_daily_weight_gain().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_ration_quality_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level diet nutritional metrics — gross metabolizable energy content, digestibility, nitrogen content, urinary energy losses, ash content — cohort-level feed ration composition shares feed component nutrient parameters. module joins ration shares feed parameters feed_id, uses species_short species-specific lookups, computes ration-weighted nutritional metrics cohort following steps: Species-specific digestibility fractions computed energy parameters using calc_feed_digestibility_fraction(). gross energy — calc_ration_gross_energy() nitrogen — calc_ration_nitrogen_content() digestibility — calc_ration_digestibility() metabolizable energy — calc_ration_metabolizable_energy() urinary energy fraction — calc_ration_urinary_energy_fraction() ash — calc_ration_ash() Component contributions summed produce cohort-level diet metrics, including calc_ration_intake().

what: Metabolic Energy Requirements and Ration Intake Module
title: About gleam modules
text: See run_metabolic_energy_req_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level daily energy requirements (MJ/head/day) feed dry matter intake (kg DM/head/day) applying IPCC Tier 2 energy partitioning functions. Energy requirements expressed net energy (NE) CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS, metabolizable energy () CML, PGS, CHK. module computes following energy partitions: Maintenance — calc_metabolic_energy_req_maintenance() Activity — calc_metabolic_energy_req_activity() Growth — calc_metabolic_energy_req_growth() Lactation — calc_metabolic_energy_req_lactation() Pregnancy — calc_metabolic_energy_req_pregnancy() Work (draught power; CTL, BFL, CML ) — calc_metabolic_energy_req_work() Fibre production (SHP, GTS, CML ) — calc_metabolic_energy_req_fibre() Total energy requirements aggregated using calc_total_metabolic_energy_req(), dry matter intake derived using calc_ration_intake() dividing total energy requirement diet energy density.

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_emissions_enteric_module() full documentation.  calculation pipeline consists following steps: ch4_mitigation_factor provided input data, set 1 (mitigation). methane conversion factor (ym) computed using calc_conversion_factor_ym(). Daily enteric methane emissions computed using calc_ch4_enteric().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_nitrogen_balance_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level daily nitrogen intake, retention, excretion (kg N/head/day) following IPCC Tier 2 approach. following calculation sequence applied: Daily nitrogen intake computed ration_intake ration_nitrogen using calc_nitrogen_intake(). Daily nitrogen retention (body tissues products growth, pregnancy, milk) computed using calc_nitrogen_retention(). Daily nitrogen excretion computed intake minus retention using calc_nitrogen_excretion().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_emissions_manure_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level greenhouse gas emissions manure management systems (MMS) following IPCC Tier 2 methodology, using volatile solids (VS), MMS allocation fractions, MMS-specific emission factors. herd_id, set MMS identifiers must consistent manure_management_system_fraction manure_management_system_factors tables. following calculation sequence applied: Volatile solids (VS) excretion computed feed ration nutritional parameters (digestibility, urinary energy, ash) using Overview GLEAM manure emissions module functions, input output: Methane (CH₄) emissions manure management computed VS MMS-specific factors (MCF B₀), reported MMS group (pasture, burned, ) using calc_ch4_manure() (IPCC 2006/2019, Eq. 10.23). Direct N₂O emissions computed nitrogen excretion MMS-specific EF3 values, reported MMS group using calc_n2o_manure_direct() (IPCC 2006/2019, Eq. 10.25). Indirect N₂O emissions sum : Volatilisation-driven N₂O MMS-specific nitrogen losses (FracGas) EF4 using calc_n2o_manure_volatilization() (IPCC 2006/2019, Eq. 10.26–10.28). Leaching/runoff-driven N₂O MMS-specific nitrogen losses (FracLeach) EF5 using calc_n2o_manure_leaching() (IPCC 2006/2019, Eq. 10.27–10.29). Total N₂O (direct + indirect) summed MMS group using calc_n2o_manure_total().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_emissions_ration_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level average greenhouse gas emission factors feed production weighting emission factors individual feed components diet composition. Returns diet-level average GHG emission factors gas emission source cohort. module joins ration shares feed emission factors feed_id applies following calculation sequence: Merge ration shares emission factors feed-component level. CO₂ fertilizer manufacture — calc_co2_ration_fertilizer() CO₂ pesticide manufacture — calc_co2_ration_pesticides() CO₂ -field crop activities — calc_co2_ration_crop_activities() CO₂ land-use change (peat) — calc_co2_ration_luc_nopeat() CO₂ land-use change (peat) — calc_co2_ration_luc_peat() N₂O fertilizer use — calc_n2o_ration_fertilizer() N₂O manure applied soil — calc_n2o_ration_manure() N₂O crop residues — calc_n2o_ration_crop_residues() CH₄ rice cultivation — calc_ch4_ration_rice() Sum component contributions within cohort obtain diet-level average emission factors (g gas/kg DM).

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_production_module() full documentation. Computes cohort-level production outputs assessment period combining cohort-level herd structure inputs herd-level production parameters. module returns milk, fibre, meat outputs cohort. following calculation sequence applied: Milk outputs (raw mass, protein, fat-protein-corrected milk — FPCM) computed using calc_milk_production(). Fibre outputs (wool, cashmere, mohair) computed using calc_fibre_production(). Meat outputs (live weight, carcass weight, bone-free meat, protein) computed using calc_meat_production().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_allocation_module() full documentation. pipeline consists following steps: Meat — calc_meat_allocation_energy() Milk — calc_milk_allocation_energy() Fibre — calc_fibre_allocation_energy() Work (draught power) — calc_work_allocation_energy() Eggs — set 0 (yet implemented) Cohort-level energy terms aggregated herd level using calc_cohort_to_herd_aggregation(). Herd-level allocation shares computed using calc_allocation_shares(). Allocation shares reshaped long format assigned emission sources using assign_allocation_shares().

title: About gleam modules
text: See run_aggregation_module() full documentation. final step GLEAM pipeline. generates final herd-level results aggregating key cohort-level outputs, scaling variables assessment duration, allocating emissions commodities, converting CH₄ N₂O emissions CO₂-equivalents (CO₂eq) using selected 100-year Global Warming Potential (GWP-100) factors. following calculation sequence applied: Cohort-level variables reshaped wide long format. Variables classified "Feed", "NitrogenBalance", "Production", "Emissions". Cohort totals calculated using calc_cohort_totals(). Production variables retained provided; emissions, feed, nitrogen balance variables scaled using cohort stock size simulation duration. Cohort totals aggregated herd level using calc_cohort_to_herd_aggregation(). Herd-level emissions merged commodity allocation shares. Emissions allocated commodities using calc_allocated_emissions(). Gas type (CH₄, N₂O, CO₂) identified emission variable name. Allocated emissions converted CO₂eq using calc_co2eq() selected GWP-100 option (AR4, AR5, AR6). Final output tables produced summarising herd-level results emissions, feed, production, nitrogen balance.

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: gleam R package implements core computational engine Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM), developed Food Agriculture Organization United Nations (FAO). provides modular, transparent, reproducible framework quantifying greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions livestock supply chains using Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) approach based IPCC Tier 2 methodology. package covers seven livestock species: cattle (CTL), buffalo (BFL), camels (CML), sheep (SHP), goats (GTS), pigs (PGS), chickens (CHK). estimates emissions enteric fermentation, manure management, feed production, allocates livestock commodities (meat, milk, fibre, work) using biophysical energy-based allocation framework consistent ISO 14044, LEAP guidelines IDF Global Carbon Footprint Standard. package part broader GLEAM ecosystem, includes interactive web applications, dashboards, data infrastructure, capacity-building tools. vignette provides overview R package specifically; brief description wider ecosystem given GLEAM Ecosystem. Species currently covered GLEAM

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: following example loads sample data shipped package runs full pipeline: already pre-computed herd structure (population sizes offtake numbers), set has_herd_structure = TRUE supply cohort-level table includes cohort_stock_size offtake_heads_assessment columns. pipeline skip herd simulation step proceed directly weight calculations.
code: library(gleam) library(data.table)  # ---- Load sample data from inst/extdata ---- path <- system.file("extdata/run_gleam_examples", package = "gleam")  cohort_dt      <- fread(file.path(path, "master_chrt_lvl_no_structure_data.csv")) herd_dt        <- fread(file.path(path, "master_hrd_lvl_data.csv")) rations_dt     <- fread(file.path(path, "feed_rations_share_chrt.csv")) feed_params_dt <- fread(file.path(path, "feed_quality.csv")) feed_emis_dt   <- fread(file.path(path, "feed_emission_factors.csv")) mms_frac_dt    <- fread(file.path(path, "manure_management_system_fraction.csv")) mms_fact_dt    <- fread(file.path(path, "manure_management_system_factors.csv"))  # ---- Run the full GLEAM pipeline ---- results <- run_gleam(   has_herd_structure                = FALSE,   cohort_level_data                 = cohort_dt,   herd_level_data                   = herd_dt,   feed_rations                      = rations_dt,   feed_params                       = feed_params_dt,   feed_emissions                    = feed_emis_dt,   manure_management_system_fraction = mms_frac_dt,   manure_management_system_factors  = mms_fact_dt,   simulation_duration               = 365,   global_warming_potential_set      = "AR6" )  # ---- Inspect results ---- print(results$cohort_level_results) print(results$allocation_long) print(results$aggregation_results$results_emissions)

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: run_gleam() returns named list four elements: cohort_level_results: cohort-level data.table containing original input columns plus every variable computed across pipeline, expressed per-head, per-day quantities. Calculated variables include cohort stock sizes offtake numbers, live weights, ration quality metrics, energy requirements, dry matter intake, enteric CH₄, nitrogen balance, volatile solids, manure CH₄ N₂O (MMS group), feed production emission factors, production outputs (milk, meat, fibre), allocation energies. herd_level_results: herd-level data.table containing herd-level input parameters plus, has_herd_structure = FALSE, annualized herd growth rate (growth_rate_herd). allocation_long: herd-level data.table long format one row per herd × commodity × emission source, containing biophysical allocation shares used distribute herd-level emissions commodities (Milk, Meat, Fibre, Work, Eggs, None). aggregation_results: named list four data.table elements: results_emissions: herd-level GHG emissions scaled assessment period expressed kg gas kg CO₂eq; results_feed: herd-level feed intake; results_production: herd-level production milk, meat, fibre; results_nitrogen: herd-level nitrogen balance.

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: Livestock agrifood systems GLEAM system boundary GLEAM applies cradle--processing-point Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) framework. system boundary encompasses: Upstream processes: feed crop pasture production, fertilizer input production, feed processing. -farm processes: animal husbandry, enteric fermentation, manure management, -farm energy use. Downstream processes: primary processing, storage, transport livestock products. Note: current R package implements -farm emission modules (metabolic energy requirements, enteric emissions, manure management emissions, production outputs, biophysical allocation). Feed production emissions computed using emission factors per feed component. Downstream processing modules development.

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: package implements IPCC Tier 2 methodology estimating GHG emissions livestock. Energy requirements partitioned maintenance, activity, growth, lactation, pregnancy, work, fibre production components using species-specific equations IPCC 2006 Guidelines 2019 Refinement. Total daily energy requirements used estimate dry matter intake, volatile solids excretion, enteric methane emissions. Energy requirements CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS expressed net energy (NE); CML, PGS, CHK expressed metabolizable energy ().

title: Overview of 'gleam' R Package
text: Environmental burdens allocated livestock co-products (meat, milk, fibre, work) using biophysical approach based energy required produce commodity. follows IDF Global Carbon Footprint Standard (IDF, 2022) consistent ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2). Emissions manure deposited pasture manure burned fuel assigned residual category “” (allocated commodity), line LCA cut-rules avoid double-counting upstream feed production.

Emission variables allocated across commodities, even appear emissions_vars (e.g., "ch4_manure_pasture", "ch4_manure_burned"). commodity_col Character. Total volatile solids (VS) excreted per animal per day, representing organic material livestock manure consisting biodegradable non-biodegradable fractions (kg VS/head/day). ... variable number manure management system (MMS) arguments. MMS must provided named numeric vector exactly following fields: manure_management_system_fraction Numeric. Fraction total manure excreted animals given herd cohort handled specific manure management system. Values ranges 0 1. sum fractions herd_id must equal 1. methane_conversion_factor_mcf Numeric. Methane conversion factor represents portion degree maximum methane producing capacity (\(B_0\)) effectively achieved within specific manure management system. represents extent theoretical methane yield realized based management practices environmental conditions, specifically temperature system, retention time organic material, degree anaerobic conditions present. value theoretically ranges 0 100 percent. Default values can selected Table 10.17 IPCC guidelines (IPCC 2006, 2019). ch4_max_producing_capacity_bo Numeric. Maximum methane producing capacity (\(B_0\)) manure management systems (m3 CH4 / kg VS). value region- species-specific, represents theoretical maximum methane yield per unit volatile solids.. title: Calculate methane (CH4) emissions from manure management systems — calc_ch4_manure
text: calculation follows structure IPCC Equation 10.23 methane (CH4) emission factors manure management. IPCC formulation, emissions determined combining: daily volatile solids excretion (volatile_solids) - see calc_volatile_solids, maximum methane-producing capacity (b0), methane conversion factor (mcf) manure management system, fraction manure handled system (manure_management_system_fraction). Applying IPCC conversion factor m3 CH4 kg CH4 (0.67), daily methane emissions calculated : $$   CH4 =   volatile\_solids \times b0 \times 0.67 \times   \sum \left( \frac{mcf}{100} \times manure\_management\_system\_fraction \right) $$ summation taken manure management systems included calculation. Results expressed daily resolution (kg CH4/head/day), consistent Equation 10.23 adapting original annual formulation daily basis. function part run_emissions_manure_module().

text: value Numeric. Variable value expressed (unit)/head/day non-production variables (unit)/cohort/assessment duration production variables. (cohorts=FJ, FS, FA, MJ, MS, MA). ration_intake Numeric. title: Calculate totals by cohort — calc_cohort_totals
text: Production variables already expressed cohort level entire assessment duration therefore returned unchanged. variables (emissions, feed, nitrogen balance) expressed per head per day scaled cohort stock size simulation duration obtain cohort-level totals. production variables: $$value\_total = value$$ emissions (except emissions feed), feed, nitrogen balance variables: $$value\_total = value \times cohort\_stock\_size \times simulation\_duration$$ feed emissions variables: $$value\_total = value \times ration\_intake \times cohort\_stock\_size \times simulation\_duration / 1000$$ function part run_aggregation_module().

title: Calculate methane conversion factor (ym) — calc_conversion_factor_ym

Calculate methane conversion factor (ym) — calc_conversion_factor_ym
text: species_short Character. Code identifying livestock species. Supported values include: PGS: pigs CML: camels CTL: cattle BFL: buffalo SHP: sheep GTS: goats cohort_short Character. Sex- age-specific cohort code describing production stage animals. Supported values include: FA: adult females (age first parturition) FS: sub-adult females (weaning age first parturition) FJ: juvenile females (birth weaning) MA: adult males (age first breeding) MS: sub-adult males (weaning age first breeding) MJ: juvenile males (birth weaning) ration_digestibility_fraction Numeric. Average digestibility feed ration, expressed ratio digestible (metabolizable, poultry) gross energy content (fraction).

title: Calculate methane conversion factor (ym) — calc_conversion_factor_ym
text: Numeric. Methane (CH4) conversion factor (ym), representing percentage  gross energy feed ration converted CH4 (percentage).

title: Calculate methane conversion factor (ym) — calc_conversion_factor_ym
text: ym computed using species- cohort-specific default relationships diet digestibility (Opio et al., 2013). ration_digestibility_fraction can calculated calc_ration_digestibility - see also run_ration_quality_module. CTL BFL: $$ym = 9.75 - 0.05 \times (ration\_digestibility\_fraction \times 100)$$ SHP, GTS CML: FA MA cohorts: $$ym = 9.75 - 0.05 \times (ration\_digestibility\_fraction \times 100)$$ FS MS cohorts: $$ym = 7.75 - 0.05 \times (ration\_digestibility\_fraction \times 100)$$ PGS: ym assigned fixed values cohort: FA MA cohorts: $$ym = 1.01$$ FS MS cohorts: $$ym = 0.39$$ ym returned 0 juvenile cohorts (FJ, MJ), assuming negligible enteric methane production weaning/rumen development. function part run_emissions_enteric_module().

title: Calculate fibre energy requirements (for biophysical allocation) — calc_fibre_allocation_energy
text: function provides fibre-related energy term used biophysical allocation framework apportion emissions milk co-products multifunctional livestock production systems. approach implements IDF (2022) standard, adapted Thoma Nemecek (2020), consistent FAO LEAP livestock LCA guidelines (FAO, 2016a, 2016b, 2016c) ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2). accordance ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2), known processing biophysical relationships may used assign shared inputs outputs single production unit individual products sub-units. livestock systems, includes apportioning shared feed energy use according physiological energy requirements (e.g., net energy lactation, growth, etc.). resulting process remains multifunctional, energy terms may subsequently used derive allocation factors among co-products. Total fibre-related energy assessment period computed fibre-producing species (CML, SHP, GTS) applicable cohorts ("FA", "FS", "MA", "MS"). fibre_allocation_energy calculated follows: \(energy\_allocation\_fibre = \frac{energy\_requirement\_fibre\_production}{ratio\_me\_to\_ne} \times simulation\_duration \times cohort\_stock\_size\) camels (CML), : \(energy\_allocation\_fibre = energy\_requirement\_fibre\_production \times simulation\_duration \times cohort\_stock\_size\) sheep (SHP) goats (GTS). metabolic_energy_req_fibre_production can computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_fibre (see also run_metabolic_energy_req_module). function part run_allocation_module().

title: Calculate meat energy requirements (for biophysical allocation) — calc_meat_allocation_energy
text: function provides meat-related energy term used biophysical allocation framework apportion emissions milk co-products multifunctional livestock production systems. approach implements IDF (2022) standard, adapted Thoma Nemecek (2020), consistent FAO LEAP livestock LCA guidelines (FAO, 2016a, 2016b, 2016c) ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2). accordance ISO 14044:2006, known biophysical relationships may used assign shared inputs outputs production system individual products sub-units. livestock systems, includes apportioning shared feed energy use according physiological energy requirements growth, lactation, maintenance. resulting process remains multifunctional, energy terms may subsequently used derive allocation factors among co-products. meat_allocation_energy calculated follows: \(energy\_allocation\_meat = specific\_energy\_meat \times meat\_production\_live\_weight\_cohort\) specific_energy_meat average energy required produce one kilogram live weight, accounting species- cohort-specific growth characteristics (MJ/kg live weight). meat_production_live_weight_cohort total live weight animals sold meat assessment period. can computed using calc_meat_production (see also run_production_module). Specific approaches species: CTL, BFL, CML, SHP, GTS: Growth energy calculated using species- cohort-specific biophysical relationships adapted established growth energy formulations (details calc_metabolic_energy_req_growth). PGS: Growth energy calculated function 0 returned. downstream processing, calc_allocation_shares assigns 100% allocation edible meat commodity pig systems. function part run_allocation_module().

Total meat carcass weight (excluding organs, -products dressing) produced assessment period cohort (kg/cohort/assessment period). meat_production_bone_free_meat_cohort Numeric. title: Calculate meat production — calc_meat_production
text: Meat production outputs computed follows: meat_production_live_weight_cohort computed : \(meat\_production\_live\_weight\_cohort =   offtake\_heads\_assessment \times live\_weight\_cohort\_at\_slaughter\) meat_production_carcass_weight_cohort computed : \(meat\_production\_carcass\_weight\_cohort =   meat\_production\_live\_weight\_cohort \times carcass\_dressing\_fraction\) meat_production_bone_free_meat_cohort computed : \(meat\_production\_bone\_free\_meat\_cohort =   meat\_production\_carcass\_weight\_cohort \times bone\_free\_meat\_fraction\) meat_production_protein_cohort computed : \(meat\_production\_protein\_cohort =   meat\_production\_bone\_free\_meat\_cohort \times meat\_protein\_fraction\) function part run_production_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for activity — calc_metabolic_energy_req_activity
text: species_short Character. Code identifying livestock species. Supported values include: PGS: pigs CML: camels CTL: cattle BFL: buffalo SHP: sheep GTS: goats cohort_short Character. Sex- age-specific cohort code describing production stage animals. Supported values include: FA: adult females (age first parturition) FS: sub-adult females (weaning age first parturition) FJ: juvenile females (birth weaning) MA: adult males (age first breeding) MS: sub-adult males (weaning age first breeding) MJ: juvenile males (birth weaning) metabolic_energy_req_maintenance Numeric. Energy required maintenance, defined amount energy needed keep animal equilibrium body energy neither gained lost. Expressed net energy CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS metabolizable energy CML PGS (MJ/head/day). live_weight_cohort_average Numeric. Average live weight cohort stage. Computed accounting share offtaken animals within cohort, using slaughter weight, potential final weight animals remain cohort (kg). low_activity_fraction Numeric. Proportion assessment period animal performs low-intensity movement typical stall-feeding near-field grazing, characterized minimal walking distances flat terrain  (fraction). high_activity_fraction Numeric. Proportion assessment period animal engages sustained locomotion associated herding long-distance grazing, typically involving extended walking distances /uneven hilly terrain (fraction).

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for activity — calc_metabolic_energy_req_activity
text: Numeric. Energy required activity, defined amount energy needed support animal movement physical activity (MJ/head/day). Expressed net energy CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS metabolizable energy CML PGS.

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for activity — calc_metabolic_energy_req_activity
text: approach follows IPCC Tier 2 energy partitioning method applies: \(metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity = cact \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance\) SHP GTS, activity energy calculated : \(metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity = cact \times live\_weight\_cohort\_average\) \(cact\) activity coefficient (dimensionless CTL, BFL, PGS; MJ/day/kg SHP,  GTS) reflects animal’s feeding management conditions. metabolic_energy_req_maintenance can calculated using calc_metabolic_energy_req_maintenance(). CTL, BFL, SHP GTS, \(cact\) computed weighted average activity levels assessment period account variation management grazing intensity. Specific coefficients species cohort: CTL, BFL (NRC, 1996; AFRC, 1993): low_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.17\) high_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.36\) CML (Wardeh, 2004): activity levels: \(cact = 0.1\) GTS (AFRC, 1993): low_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.019\) high_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.024\) SHP (AFRC, 1993): low_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.0107\) high_activity_fraction: \(cact = 0.024\) PGS (NRC, 1998): activity levels: \(cact = 0.125\) function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for fibre production — calc_metabolic_energy_req_fibre
text: component follows IPCC Tier 2 partitioning approach applied fibre-producing species relevant cohorts, assumed FA, FS, MA, MS. Species-specific approach: SHP GTS (IPCC, 2006; IPCC, 2019): sheep goats, fibre production energy calculated assuming fixed net energy cost \(24\) MJ per kilogram fibre produced. Annual fibre production converted daily requirement : $$ metabolic\_energy\_req\_fibre = \frac{24 \times fibre\_yield\_year}{365} $$ : \(fibre\_yield\_year\) annual fibre production (kg/head/year), \(24\) net energy requirement per kilogram fibre (MJ/kg fibre), division \(365\) converts annual production daily basis. CML (AFRC, 1998; Cannas et al., 2007): camels, fibre energy requirements first calculated net energy basis converted metabolizable energy using net--metabolizable energy efficiency coefficient: $$ metabolic\_energy\_req\_fibre = \frac{24}{0.43} \times \frac{fibre\_yield\_year}{365} $$ : \(24\) net energy requirement per kilogram fibre (MJ/kg fibre), \(0.43\) efficiency conversion metabolizable energy net energy fibre production (fraction), \(fibre\_prod\) annual fibre production per animal (kg/head/year), \(365\) convert annual production daily basis. efficiency coefficient \(0.43\) adopted analogy goats, assuming dietary metabolizability approximately 0.55, following AFRC guidance synthesis Cannas et al. (2007). species: Fibre production energy assumed zero. function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for growth — calc_metabolic_energy_req_growth
text: function follows IPCC Tier 2 energy partitioning approach applies species-specific equations growth energy requirements. general, growth energy computed growing cohorts (FJ, FS, MJ, MS); implementation, growth set 0 adult cohorts (FA, MA). Species-specific approach: CTL BFL (NRC, 1996; IPCC, 2006; IPCC, 2019) Growth energy computed using growth coefficient \(cgro\) differs castrated intact males. male cohorts, \(cgro\) calculated weighted average using offtake_rate, assuming animals removed herd castrated animals remaining cohort intact. SHP GTS (Gibbs et al., 2002; AFRC, 1993; IPCC, 2006; IPCC, 2019) sheep goats, growth energy calculated using linear formulation coefficients \(\) \(b\) (MJ/kg live weight). male cohorts, coefficients differ castrated intact males; model computes weighted average using offtake_rate, assuming offtaken animals castrated. CML (Al-Jassim, 2019) Growth energy represented using simplified linear relationship daily weight gain. PGS (NRC, 1998) pigs, growth assumed consist exclusively protein tissue fat tissue, growth energy requirements expressed metabolizable energy (). growth energy coefficient \(cgro\) (MJ/kg live weight) calculated : \(     cgro =     prot\_tissue\_frac \times meat\_protein \times meat\_protein\_energy     + (1 - prot\_tissue\_frac) \times fat\_adipose\_tissue_frac \times meat\_fat\_energy   \) Total metabolizable energy required growth : \(metabolic\_energy\_req\_growth = daily\_weight\_gain \times cgro\) : \(cgro\) growth energy coefficient (MJ/kg live weight), prot_tissue_frac = 0.65 fraction protein tissue daily weight gain, meat_protein = 0.23 fraction protein protein tissue, meat_protein_energy = 54.0 cost protein deposition (MJ/kg protein), fat_adipose_tissue_frac = 0.90 fraction fat adipose tissue, meat_fat_energy = 52.3 cost fat deposition (MJ/kg fat). function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for lactation — calc_metabolic_energy_req_lactation
text: approach follows IPCC Tier 2 partitioning method applies species-specific equations lactation energy requirements function quantity milk produced species-specific energy cost per unit milk. Requirements calculated cohort = FA (adult females) scaled proportion lactating animals (lactating_females_fraction) reproducing females (parturition_rate) within cohort. Species-specific approach: CTL, BFL, CML, SHP GTS: Total milk production includes: milk extracted human consumption (milk_yield) milk consumed directly offspring (milk_for_offspring) general form, lactation energy computed : $$ metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation = (milk\_yield \times lactating\_females\_fraction + milk\_for\_offspring) \times energy\_milk $$ : energy_milk species-specific coefficient representing net energy cost producing one kilogram milk (MJ/kg milk). Species-specific values energy_milk : CTL, BFL: estimated function milk fat content, \(1.47 + 0.40 \times (milk\_fat\_fraction \times 100)\) (NRC, 1989), CML: \(4.063\) (Wardeh, 2004), SHP: \(4.6\) (AFRC, 1993), GTS: \(3.0\) (AFRC, 1998). milk_for_offspring daily amount milk required rear offspring across year (kg/day). calculated assuming 5 kg milk required kilogram live-weight gain weaning: $$ milk\_for\_offspring = \frac{parturition\_rate \times 5 \times (weaning\_weight - birth\_weight)}{365} $$ SHP GTS, milk_for_offspring multiplied litter_size account multiple offspring per birth. PGS (NRC, 1998): Lactation energy accounts milk consumed directly offspring (milk_for_offspring), adjusted fraction reproductive cycle spent lactation (cadj): $$ \begin{aligned} metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation &= litter\_size \times (1 - 0.5 \times death\_rate\_juvenile) \times \\ & \left( \frac{0.02059 \times (weaning\_weight - birth\_weight) \times 1000} {lactation\_duration} - \frac{0.3766}{0.67} \right) \times cadj \end{aligned} $$ : \(0.02059\) coefficient lactation energy requirement (MJ/g live weight), \(0.3766\) coefficient sow weight loss lactation (MJ/head/day), \(0.67\) efficiency conversion dietary intake milk energy (fraction), \(cadj\) fraction reproductive cycle spent lactation: $$   cadj = \frac{lactation\_duration}{non\_productive\_duration + pregnancy\_duration + lactation\_duration}   $$ function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for maintenance — calc_metabolic_energy_req_maintenance
text: approach follows IPCC Tier 2 partitioning method applies: \(metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance = cmain \times average\_weight^{0.75}\) \(cmain\) category-specific coefficient (MJ/day/kg\(^{0.75}\)) reflects basal metabolic requirements varies species, physiological status, sex. selected cohorts, \(cmain\) computed weighted average account : CTL, BFL: lactating vs. non-lactating females CTL, BFL, SHP: intact vs. castrated males - Offtaken animals assumed castrated SHP: animals vs. one year age Specific coefficients species cohort: CTL BFL (NRC, 1996; AFRC, 1993): FA: \(cmain = 0.386 \times lactating\_females\_fraction + 0.322 \times (1 - lactating\_females\_fraction)\) FS, FJ, MJ: \(cmain = 0.322\) MA, MS: \(cmain = 0.322 \times offtake\_rate + 0.370 \times (1 - offtake\_rate)\) CML (Wardeh, 2004): cohorts: \(cmain = 0.435\) GTS (AFRC, 1993): cohorts: \(cmain = 0.315\) SHP (AFRC, 1993): FA: \(cmain = 0.217\) FJ: \(cmain = 0.236\) FS: \(cmain = 0.236 \times (365/age\_first\_parturition) +          0.217 \times ((age\_first\_parturition - 365)/age\_first\_parturition)\) MA: \(cmain = 0.217 \times offtake\_rate + (0.217 \times 1.15) \times (1 - offtake\_rate)\) MJ: \(cmain = 0.236 \times offtake\_rate + (0.236 \times 1.15) \times (1 - offtake\_rate)\) MS: \(cmain =       (0.217 \times offtake\_rate + (0.217 \times 1.15) \times (1 - offtake\_rate)) \times ((age\_first\_parturition - 365)/age\_first\_parturition) +       (0.236 \times offtake\_rate + (0.236 \times 1.15) \times (1 - offtake\_rate)) \times (365/age\_first\_parturition)\) PGS (NRC, 1998): cohorts: \(cmain = 0.4435\) function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate metabolic energy requirements for pregnancy — calc_metabolic_energy_req_pregnancy
text: component follows IPCC Tier 2 partitioning framework applied female cohorts (FA FS). Pregnancy energy (metabolic_energy_req_pregnancy) represents additional energy required support gestation. Requirements computed fraction maintenance energy adjusted reflect reproductive activity within cohort: FA, requirements scaled annual parturition rate (parturition_rate) (applicable) fraction reproductive cycle spent gestation (pregnancy_duration/(pregnancy_duration+lactation_duration+non_productive_duration)). FS, fraction animals assumed reach reproductive age within cohort; requirements therefore scaled proportion remaining cohort (\(1 - offtake\_rate\)) share cohort duration spent pregnant (pregnancy_duration/cohort_duration_days). General form $$ metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy = metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times c_{preg} \times S $$ \(c_{preg}\) species-specific pregnancy coefficient, \(S\) scaling term depends cohort (FA vs FS). \(metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance\) can calculated using calc_metabolic_energy_req_maintenance() Specific coefficients species cohort: CTL BFL (IPCC, 2006, 2019): Pregnancy energy approximated \(10\%\) maintenance energy. FA: $$   \begin{aligned}   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &=   0.10 \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times parturition\_rate \times \\   & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{365}   \end{aligned} $$ FS: $$   \begin{aligned}   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &=   0.10 \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times \\   & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{cohort\_duration\_days} \times   (1 - offtake\_rate)   \end{aligned} $$ CML (Wardeh, 2004): Pregnancy energy estimated \(12\%\) maintenance energy. FA: $$   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy =   0.12 \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times parturition\_rate   $$ FS: $$   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy =   0.12 \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times   \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{cohort\_duration\_days} \times (1 - offtake\_rate)   $$ SHP GTS (IPCC 2006; 2019): Pregnancy energy calculated litter-size-dependent fraction maintenance energy (\(c_{preg}\)). FA: $$   \begin{aligned}   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &=   metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times c_{preg} \times parturition\_rate \times \\   & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{365}   \end{aligned}   $$ \(c_{preg}\) : $$   c_{preg} =   \left\{   \begin{array}{ll}   0.077 \times (2 - litter\_size) + 0.126 \times (litter\_size - 1), & 1 \le litter\_size \le 2 \\   0.150, & litter\_size > 2   \end{array}   \right.   $$ FS: single-birth coefficient used (\(c_{preg}=0.077\)) scaled proportion reproductive individuals cohort: $$   \begin{aligned}   metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &=   0.12 \times metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance \times \\   & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{cohort\_duration\_days} \times \\   & (1 - offtake\_rate)   \end{aligned} $$ PGS (NRC, 1998): Pregnancy energy expressed using gestation coefficient \(c_{gest}\) (MJ/piglet), default \(c_{gest}=0.14985\). FA: $$ \begin{aligned} metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &= c_{gest} \times litter\_size \times \\ & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{ non\_productive\_duration + pregnancy\_duration + lactation\_duration } \end{aligned} $$ FS: $$ \begin{aligned} metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy &= c_{gest} \times litter\_size \times \\ & \frac{pregnancy\_duration}{cohort\_duration\_days} \times \\ & (1 - offtake\_rate) \end{aligned} $$ function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate milk production — calc_milk_production
text: Milk production outputs computed follows: milk_production_mass_cohort computed : \(milk\_production =   milk\_yield\_day \times simulation\_duration \times cohort\_stock\_size \times lactating\_females\_fraction\) milk_production_protein_cohort computed : \(milk\_protein\_production =   milk\_production \times milk\_protein\_fraction\) milk_production_fpcm_cohort computed using ratio energy content actual versus standard milk: \(FPCM = milk\_production \times \frac{E_{milk}}{E_{standard}}\) milk energy content (Mcal/kg) computed (IDF, 2022): \(E_{milk} =   0.0929 \times milk\_fat\_fraction +   0.0547 \times milk\_protein\_fraction +   0.0395 \times milk\_lactose\_fraction\) \(E_{standard} =   0.0929 \times milk\_fat\_fraction\_standard +   0.0547 \times milk\_protein\_fraction\_standard +   0.0395 \times milk\_lactose\_fraction\_standard\) Non-zero milk outputs expected adult female cohorts milk-producing species. function part run_production_module().

text: calculation follows Tier 2 methodology direct nitrous oxide (N2O) emissions manure management defined IPCC Guidelines (Equation 10.25). Daily emissions computed : $$ \begin{aligned} N2O &= nitrogen\_excretion \times ratio\_N2ON\_to\_N2O \times \\ & \sum \left( manure\_management\_system\_fraction \times n2o\_ef3 \right) \end{aligned} $$ function part run_emissions_manure_module().

title: Calculate indirect Nitrous Oxide (N2O) emissions from manure volatilization — calc_n2o_manure_volatilization
text: calculation follows Tier 2 methodology indirect N2O emissions manure management defined IPCC Guidelines Equations 10.26 (IPCC, 2006, 2019), 10.27 (IPCC, 2006) 10.28 (IPCC, 2019). IPCC formulation, indirect emissions atmospheric deposition volatilised nitrogen calculated : $$   N2O_{G(mm)} =   \frac{44}{28}   \sum_{S} \left(     N \times AWMS_S \times FracGas_{S} \times EF4   \right) $$ : \(N2O_G(mm)\) Indirect N2O emissions due volatilization N Manure Management. \(44/28\) Conversion factor N2O-N N2O. \(N\) Nitrogen excreted (kg N). \(AWMS_S\) Fraction excreted nitrogen managed manure management system \(S\). \(FracGas_{S}\) Fraction nitrogen volatilised NH3–N NOx–N manure management system \(S\). \(EF4\) Emission factor indirect N2O emissions atmospheric deposition (kg N2O-N per kg NH3–N + NOx–N). implementation, calculations performed daily, per-head resolution using nitrogen_excretion (kg N/head/day): $$ \begin{aligned} N_2O &= nitrogen\_excretion \times ratio\_N2ON\_to\_N2O \times \\ & \sum_{S} \left( manure\_management\_system\_fraction \times nitrogen\_fracgas \times n2o\_ef4 \right) \end{aligned} $$ function part run_emissions_manure_module().

text: species_short Character. Code identifying livestock species. Supported values include: PGS: pigs CML: camels CTL: cattle BFL: buffalo SHP: sheep GTS: goats cohort_short Character. Sex- age-specific cohort code describing production stage animals. Supported values include: FA: adult females (age first parturition) FS: sub-adult females (weaning age first parturition) FJ: juvenile females (birth weaning) MA: adult males (age first breeding) MS: sub-adult males (weaning age first breeding) MJ: juvenile males (birth weaning) milk_protein_fraction Numeric. Milk protein fraction (kg protein / kg milk). Required species = CML, CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS. milk_yield_day Numeric. Average milk yield per milk-producing animal assessment duration (kg/head/day). value calculated total quantity milk produced human consumption milk-producing animals assessment period, divided number milk-producing animals, length assessment period (days). Required species = CML, CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS. daily_weight_gain Numeric. Average live weight gain cohort cohort stage (kg/head/day). fibre_yield_year Numeric. Annual production yield fibre, wool, cashmere, mohair (kg/head/year). Required species = CML, SHP, GTS. litter_size Numeric. Average number offspring born per parturition (# offsprings/parturition). value can calculated total number offspring born divided total number parturitions year. parturition_rate Numeric. Average annual number parturitions per female animal (# parturitions/adult female/year). herd-level reproductive performance indicator calculated total number parturitions (deliveries) occurring year divided number adult females potentially able give birth year. live_weight_at_weaning Numeric. Live weight animal weaning (kg). live_weight_at_birth Numeric. Live weight animal birth (kg). pregnancy_duration Numeric. Duration pregnancy period (days). cohort_duration_days Numeric. title: Calculate daily nitrogen retention — calc_nitrogen_retention
text: Species-specific nitrogen retention calculations applied. CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS, CML: Nitrogen retained products tissues computed consistent process described Technical paper MPI (Ministry Primary Industries (MPI), 2025), nitrogen retention calculated sum : nitrogen secreted milk, nitrogen retained live weight gain (tissue), nitrogen retained fibre (fibre-producing species). Coefficients nitrogen content deposited tissue, fibre, milk derived Chapter 5 (Nitrogen Excretion) MPI Technical paper. following constants used: Tissue nitrogen content (tissue_n) CTL BFL: 0.0326 kg N/kg live weight SHP, GTS CML: 0.026 kg N/kg live weight Fibre nitrogen content (fibre_n) SHP, GTS CML: 0.134 kg N/kg fibre Milk nitrogen content (milk_n): CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS CML: derived milk_protein_fraction using protein--nitrogen conversion factor 6.25 kg protein/kg nitrogen PGS Nitrogen retention calculated following IPCC (2019) Tier 2 equations swine (Equations 10.33A 10.33B). Nitrogen retention includes nitrogen retained : live weight gain (tissue), reproductive outputs (conceptus weaned offspring). implementation: Nitrogen content live weight gain assumed 0.025 kg N/kg live weight. Protein digestibility fraction assumed  0.98 (dimensionless). breeding cohorts, reproductive component represented annual nitrogen retention conceptus weaned offspring, expressed daily average distributing annual value uniformly year (365 days). constant factor  0.806 (dimensionless) applied piglets' live weight gain correct higher nitrogen content per unit live weight gain, following IPCC (2019). function part run_nitrogen_balance_module().

title: Calculate total metabolic energy requirements — calc_total_metabolic_energy_req
text: component follows IPCC Tier 2 partitioning approach calculation computed differently depending whether species energy requirements expressed net metabolizable energy. Species-specific approach: Energy requirements expressed net energy (CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS) CTL BFL: $$       metabolic\_energy\_req\_total =       \frac{         \left(           \frac{             metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_work +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy           }{REM}         \right)         +         \left(           \frac{metabolic\_energy\_req\_growth}{REG}         \right)       }{diet\_digestibility\_fraction}     $$ SHP GTS: $$       metabolic\_energy\_req\_total =       \frac{         \left(           \frac{             metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy           }{REM}         \right)         +         \left(           \frac{             metabolic\_energy\_req\_growth +             metabolic\_energy\_req\_fibre           }{REG}         \right)       }{diet_digestibility_fraction}     $$ Energy requirements expressed metabolizable energy (CML, PGS) species, total daily requirement computed direct sum relevant energy components (MJ/head/day). CML: $$       metabolic\_energy\_req\_total =       metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_work +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_fibre\_production +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_growth     $$ PGS: $$       metabolic\_energy\_req\_total =       metabolic\_energy\_req\_maintenance +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_activity +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_lactation +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_pregnancy +       metabolic\_energy\_req\_growth     $$ function part run_metabolic_energy_req_module().

title: Calculate Volatile Solids (VS) — calc_volatile_solids
text: IPCC recommends estimating volatile solids (VS) excretion feed intake digestibility country-specific average daily VS excretion rates available. core relationship provided IPCC Equation 10.24 (Volatile solids excretion rates), estimates daily VS excretion function : Gross energy intake (gross_energy_intake, MJ/day) Digestibility diet (ration_digestibility_fraction) Urinary energy expressed fraction GE (ration_urinary_energy_fraction) Ash content diet (ration_ash, fraction dry matter) conversion factor representing average gross energy content dry matter (18.45 MJ/kg DM) general structure Eq. 10.24 partitions gross energy intake digestible energy, urinary losses, ash, converts remaining organic matter volatile solids using energy density dry matter. Implementation note. function applies algebraically simplified formulation Equation 10.24 IPCC. implementation, function takes ration_intake directly input. can calculated upstream calc_ration_intake function energy requirements energy content diet. $$   dry\_matter\_intake = \frac{gross\_energy\_intake}{diet\_gross\_energy} $$ reflects use ration-specific energy content upstream avoids assuming fixed gross energy density 18.45 MJ/kg DM, IPCC default approach. volatile solids excretion calculated : $$ volatile\_solids = dry\_matter\_intake \times (1 - diet\_digestibility\_fraction + urinary\_energy\_fraction) \times (1 - diet\_ash) $$ resulting calculations algebraically equivalent IPCC approach fully consistent Equation 10.24. function part run_emissions_manure_module().

title: Calculate work energy requirements (for biophysical allocation) — calc_work_allocation_energy
text: function provides work-related energy term used biophysical allocation framework apportion emissions milk co-products multifunctional livestock production systems. approach implements IDF (2022) standard, adapted Thoma Nemecek (2020), consistent FAO LEAP livestock LCA guidelines (FAO, 2016a, 2016b, 2016c) ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2). accordance ISO 14044:2006 (Section 4.3.4.2, Step 2), known processing biophysical relationships may used assign shared inputs outputs single production unit individual products sub-units. livestock systems, includes apportioning shared feed energy use according physiological energy requirements (e.g., net energy lactation, growth, etc.). resulting process remains multifunctional, energy terms may subsequently used derive allocation factors among co-products. Total work-related energy computed species (CTL, BFL, CML) cohorts (, FA, MA) assumed potentially involved draught power generation. work_allocation_energy calculated follows: \(energy\_allocation\_work = energy\_requirement\_work \times simulation\_duration \times cohort\_stock\_size\) cattle (CTL) buffalo (BFL), : \(energy\_allocation\_work = \frac{energy\_requirement\_work \times simulation\_duration \times cohort\_stock\_size} {ratio\_me\_to\_ne}\) camels (CML). metabolic_energy_req_work can computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_work (see also run_metabolic_energy_req_module). function part run_allocation_module().

Includes gas type, allocation shares, commodity metadata, GWP factors, emissions expressed allocated gas mass (kg gas) CO2-equivalents (kg CO2eq). results_feed data.table containing herd-level feed variables, aggregated herd level scaled assessment duration. results_production data.table containing herd-level production variables aggregated cohort-level values assessment duration. results_nitrogen data.table containing herd-level nitrogen balance variables aggregated cohort-level values scaled assessment duration.

title: Run Aggregation Module Pipeline — run_aggregation_module
text: function represents final step Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() performs following calculation sequence: Cohort-level variables reshaped wide long format. Variables classified "Feed", "NitrogenBalance", "Production", "Emissions". Cohort totals calculated using calc_cohort_totals(). Production variables retained provided, whereas emissions, feed, nitrogen balance variables scaled using cohort stock size simulation duration. Cohort totals aggregated herd level within herd_id x species_short x variable_type x variable_name group. Herd-level emissions merged commodity allocation shares allocation_herd_long. Emissions allocated commodities using calc_allocated_emissions(). Gas type identified emission variable name "CH4", "N2O", "CO2". Allocated CH4, N2O, CO2 emissions converted CO2-equivalents (CO2eq) using calc_co2eq() selected GWP-100 option. Final output tables produced summarizing herd-level results emissions, feed, production, nitrogen balance variables.

title: Run Allocation Module Pipeline — run_allocation_module
text: function implements allocation pipeline used derive biophysical allocation shares livestock commodities multifunctional production systems. approach follows IDF standard dairy sector, adapted livestock systems emissions apportioned among multiple products according physiological energy requirements. accordance ISO 14044:2006, known biophysical relationships may used assign shared inputs outputs production system individual products sub-units. function represents intermediate allocation module Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() performs following calculation sequence: Calculation cohort-level energy allocation terms meat, milk, fibre, work, eggs using calc_meat_allocation_energy, calc_milk_allocation_energy, calc_fibre_allocation_energy, calc_work_allocation_energy, calc_eggs_allocation_energy. Aggregation cohort-level energy terms herd level using calc_cohort_to_herd_aggregation. Calculation herd-level allocation shares commodities using calc_allocation_shares. Reshaping allocation shares long format assignment shares emission sources using assign_allocation_shares. Commodity-specific allocation shares represent fraction total herd-level energy requirements attributable commodity. shares used assign emissions meat, milk, fibre, work, eggs, residual category "None". Emissions manure burned fuel manure deposited pasture allocated livestock commodities. flows assigned fully "None" accordance rules implemented assign_allocation_shares.

Supported values include: FA: adult females (age first parturition) FS: sub-adult females (weaning age first parturition) FJ: juvenile females (birth weaning) MA: adult males (age first breeding) MS: sub-adult males (weaning age first breeding) MJ: juvenile males (birth weaning) cohort_duration_days Numeric vector length 6. Annual mortality offtake inputs converted daily hazards daily transition probabilities competing risks (death vs. offtake vs. survival). Conceptually, corresponds steady-state demographic approach implemented Dynmod STEADY1 (Lesnoff, 2013), adapted daily time-step formulation within R workflow fully integrated GLEAM computational pipeline.

Fraction manure nitrogen excreted given livestock category lost leaching runoff specific manure management system. parameter highly uncertain used estimate indirect N2O emissions nitrogen enters surrounding environment storage facility. expressed dimensionless fraction (0–1). title: Run Emissions from Manure Module Pipeline — run_emissions_manure_module
text: function represents intermediate module Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() estimate emissions manure management systems (MMS) orchestrates cohort-level implementation IPCC manure management methodology. following calculation sequence applied: VS excretion computed nutritional parameters feed ration (digestibility, urinary energy, ash) using simplified formulation Equation 10.24 (IPCC 2006, 2019) - calc_volatile_solids Methane (CH4) emissions manure management computed VS MMS-specific factors (MCF B0) reported MMS group (pasture, burned, ), consistently Equation 10.23 (IPCC, 2006, 2019) - calc_ch4_manure Direct nitrous oxide (N2O) emissions manure management computed nitrogen excretion MMS-specific EF3 values, reported MMS group, consistently Equation 10.25 (IPCC, 2006, 2019) -  calc_n2o_manure_direct Indirect N2O emissions computed sum : volatilisation-driven N2O using MMS-specific nitrogen losses (FracGas) EF4, consistently Equations 10.26 (IPCC, 2006, 2019), 10.27 (IPCC, 2006), 10.28 (IPCC, 2019) - calc_n2o_manure_volatilization leaching/runoff-driven N2O using MMS-specific nitrogen losses (FracLeach) EF5, consistently Equations 10.28 (IPCC, 2006), 10.27 (IPCC, 2019), 10.29 (IPCC, 2006, 2019) - calc_n2o_manure_leaching Total N2O emissions aggregated MMS group (pasture, burned, ) - calc_n2o_manure_total approach corresponds Tier 2 implementation : VS derived ration-level inputs rather using fixed daily excretion defaults, emissions allocated across MMS categories using herd/cohort MMS fractions MMS-specific parameters.

title: Run Emissions from Feed Production Module Pipeline — run_emissions_ration_module
text: function represents intermediate module Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() estimate emissions feed production used animal's ration. function joins rations_share feed_emissions feed_id, uses species_short directly, computes ration-weighted emission factors cohort. following calculation sequence applied: Merge ration shares emission factors feed-component level using merge feed_id (left join: .x = TRUE). Compute feed-component contributions (row-wise) emission source multiplying ration share feed component (feed_ration_fraction) corresponding feed emission factor. contribution computed using specific helper (called = .): CO2 fertilizer: calc_co2_ration_fertilizer CO2 pesticides: calc_co2_ration_pesticides CO2 crop operations: calc_co2_ration_crop_activities CO2 land-use change (peat): calc_co2_ration_luc_nopeat CO2 land-use change (peat): calc_co2_ration_luc_peat N2O fertilizer: calc_n2o_ration_fertilizer N2O manure applied: calc_n2o_ration_manure N2O crop residues: calc_n2o_ration_crop_residues CH4 rice cultivation: calc_ch4_ration_rice Aggregate cohort-level diet emission factors summing feed-component contributions across feeds within group (herd_id, species_short, cohort_short). emission source, cohort-level dietary emission factors computed : $$   \mathrm{diet\_ef} =   \sum_{=1}^{n}   \left(     \mathrm{feed\_ration\_fraction}_{}     \times     \mathrm{feed\_ef}_{}   \right) $$

title: Run Emissions from Feed Production Module Pipeline — run_emissions_ration_module
code: # \donttest{ # Load cleaned example input from the package and compute the calculation of feed emission factors  # Load table with ration shares rations_share <- data.table::fread(system.file(   "extdata/run_modules_examples/feed_rations_share_chrt.csv",   package = "gleam" ))  # Load table with feed emission factors feed_emissions <- data.table::fread(system.file(   "extdata/run_modules_examples/feed_emission_factors.csv",   package = "gleam" ))  # Run the code result <- run_emissions_ration_module(   rations_share = rations_share,   feed_emissions = feed_emissions ) #> 🕒 Aggregating feed emissions, please wait… #> ✔ Feed emissions aggregation complete. # }

title: Run the full Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) Pipeline — run_gleam
text: Runs full GLEAM pipeline master herd cohort inputs modules: herd (optional), weights, ration quality, energy requirements, manure emissions, enteric fermentation, nitrogren balance, feed emissions, allocation, aggregation. Common identifiers: Several input tables share following identifier columns. supported values listed referenced throughout. species_short — Character. Species code: CTL: Cattle BFL: Buffalo SHP: Sheep GTS: Goats PGS: Pigs CML: Camels cohort_short — Character. Sex- age-specific cohort code: FA: adult females (age first parturition) FS: sub-adult females (weaning age first parturition) FJ: juvenile females (birth weaning) MA: adult males (age first breeding) MS: sub-adult males (weaning age first breeding) MJ: juvenile males (birth weaning)

title: Run the full Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) Pipeline — run_gleam
text: GLEAM package implements core computational engine Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM), developed Food Agriculture Organization United Nations (FAO). provides modular workflow quantifying greenhouse gas emissions livestock systems using Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) approach based IPCC Tier 2 methodology. pipeline covers seven species (CTL, BFL, CML, SHP, GTS, PGS). Within herd, animals organised six sex-age cohorts (FJ, FS, FA, MJ, MS, MA). identifiers used consistently across modules. assessment period specified days via simulation_duration (typically 365). Intermediate per-head-per-day variables carried cohort workflow scaled cohort herd totals final aggregation step. inputs containing herd_id must refer herd set. Validation blocks variables expected produced internally.

title: Run the full Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) Pipeline — run_gleam
text: has_herd_structure = FALSE, generate herd structure run_demographic_herd_module; otherwise use supplied tables directly. Compute cohort weights (run_weights_module). Summarise ration quality (run_ration_quality_module) merge cohort table. Compute energy requirements dry matter intake (run_metabolic_energy_req_module). Compute enteric CH4 (run_emissions_enteric_module). Compute nitrogen balance (run_nitrogen_balance_module). Compute manure emissions (run_emissions_manure_module). Summarise feed production emissions (run_emissions_ration_module) merge cohort table. Compute production outputs (run_production_module). Compute allocation (run_allocation_module). Aggregate herd-level results CO2-eq (run_aggregation_module).

title: Run Metabolic Energy Requirements and Dry Matter Intake Module Pipeline — run_metabolic_energy_req_module
text: function joins cohort_level_data herd_level_data herd_id, uses species_short directly species-specific energy calculations, computes IPCC Tier 2 energy partition components derived feed intake metrics cohort. Energy requirements expressed : Net energy CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS. Metabolizable energy CML PGS. function represents intermediate module Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() estimate animals' metabolic energy requirements dry matter intake performs following calculation sequence: Maintenance energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_maintenance. Activity energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_activity. Growth energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_growth. Lactation energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_lactation. Work energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_work. Fibre production energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_fibre. Pregnancy energy computed using calc_metabolic_energy_req_pregnancy. Diet net energy ratios computed using calc_rem_maintenance calc_reg_growth (ruminants ). Total daily energy requirement computed using calc_total_metabolic_energy_req. Daily dry matter intake computed using calc_ration_intake.

title: Run Weights Module Pipeline — run_weights_module
text: function represents intermediate module Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline run_gleam() estimate animals' live weight composed following steps: Cohort-stage weight assignment using calc_cohort_weights. Herd-level biological parameters matched cohort row herd_id via data.table joins. Calculation average final live weights (accounting offtake) using calc_avg_weights. Calculation average daily live weight gain using calc_daily_weight_gain.

title: gleam 0.8.0
text: Full pipeline function run_gleam() integrating modules end--end, supporting cohort-input pre-computed herd-structure workflows. Complete modular API: run_demographic_herd_module(), run_weights_module(), run_ration_quality_module(), run_metabolic_energy_req_module(), run_nitrogen_balance_module(), run_emissions_enteric_module(), run_emissions_manure_module(), run_emissions_ration_module(), run_production_module(), run_allocation_module(), run_aggregation_module(). Support AR5 AR6 global warming potential sets run_aggregation_module(). Bundled example datasets inst/extdata/ modules full pipeline. Four vignettes covering package overview, module descriptions, ecosystem context, GLEAM history.
